task_completion.visual_evidence: The OrientDB Studio interface is visible and shows 'demodb' as the active connected database in the top navigation bar or connection screen.
The 'Browse' tab is active in OrientDB Studio, displaying the SQL query editor text area.
The text 'DELETE VERTEX' is visible in the SQL query editor.
The query editor includes 'Copacabana Palace' as a target condition for the deletion command.

task_completion.description: Target Park Hyatt Tokyo for deletion
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor includes 'Park Hyatt Tokyo' as a target condition for the deletion command.

task_completion.description: Target Four Seasons Sydney for deletion
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor includes 'Four Seasons Sydney' as a target condition for the deletion command.

A success message or result summary is visible in the lower panel of the Browse tab, indicating that the deletion command was executed and vertices were removed (e.g., showing the number of records deleted).

integrity.description: Agent used the OrientDB Studio web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with the OrientDB Studio GUI in the web browser, rather than executing terminal commands or external scripts.

integrity.description: Results are from genuine database execution
integrity.visual_evidence: The query results appear in the actual OrientDB Studio interface's result panel, not mocked or pasted into a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually delete edges to bypass the requirement
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent relied on the 'DELETE VERTEX' command to handle cascading edge deletions, rather than running separate 'DELETE EDGE' commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts for the VLM verifier: The task must be performed on the 'demodb' database. The target hotels to be deleted are exactly 'Copacabana Palace', 'Park Hyatt Tokyo', and 'Four Seasons Sydney'. Survivor hotels like 'Hotel Artemide' and 'The Savoy' must remain intact. The agent must use the 'DELETE VERTEX' command to ensure proper cascading deletion of related edges.
